'''Leon Ruan''' (born 14 May 2003) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Betfred Super League]], on a season-long loan from [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

He previously played for [[Doncaster RLFC|Doncaster]] in [[RFL League One]] and the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]]. Ruan spent time on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Doncaster RLFC|Doncaster]] and the [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] as well as [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League|top flight]]. He spent time on loan from [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] at [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the Super League.

==Background==
Ruan was born in [[Leeds, West Yorkshire]], England and grew up in [[Morley, West Yorkshire]].<ref>[https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/introducing-leon-ruan-leeds-rhinos-fan-hoping-to-earn-playing-contract-with-hometown-club-3820292 Introducing Leon Ruan: Leeds Rhinos fan hoping to earn playing contract with hometown club]</ref>

He played for [[Batley Boys]] as a junior.<ref>[https://www.batleyboysarlfc.com/news/batley-boys-u12s-narrow-loss-to-kippax-1415572.html Batley Boys u12's narrow loss to Kippax]</ref>

Ruan was part of [[Wakefield Trinity]]'s Academy system.<ref>[https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/bradford-bulls-land-hull-kr-1570218 Bradford Bulls land Hull KR forward in latest recruitment move]</ref>

==Career==
Ruan made his debut for [[Doncaster RLFC|Doncaster]] in February 2022 against [[Whitehaven RLFC|Whitehaven]] in the [[2022 RFL League 1|League 1]].

His 2022 season with the [[Doncaster RLFC|Dons]] earned him a move to the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]], signing a three-year deal from the following season.<ref>[https://www.doncasterrugbyleague.co.uk/article/1244/news-%7C-leon-ruan-signs-for-leeds-rhinos NEWS | Leon Ruan signs for Leeds Rhinos]</ref>

In March 2024 Ruan was sent on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] to Doncaster in [[2024 RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref>[https://www.doncasterrugbyleague.co.uk/article/1682/news-%7C-leon-ruan-signs-for-dons NEWS | Leon Ruan signs for Dons]</ref>

He played two games on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Rhinos]] at the [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[2024 RFL Championship|second tier]] in May and June 2024.<ref>[https://www.therhinos.co.uk/article/20782/loan-watch-johnson-and-ruan-in-action-for-halifax-and-hull- Loan Watch: Johnson and Ruan in action for Halifax and Hull]</ref>

Ruan spent more time away from [[Headingley]], spending the final few months of the [[2024 Super League season]] on loan at [[Hull FC]].<ref>[https://www.hullfc.com/blog/2024/08/01/ruan-joins-on-loan/ Ruan Joins On Loan]</ref>

He joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on a two-year deal ahead of the [[2025 Super League season]].<ref>[https://hullkr.co.uk/news/leon-ruan-signs-two-year-deal-at-hull-kr-after-successful-trial Leon Ruan signs two-year deal at Hull KR after successful trial]</ref>

Ruan spent time on loan from [[Hull KR]] at [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[2025 RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref>[https://www.fevarchive.co.uk/players/leon-ruan/1238/ Ruan, Leon]</ref>

He spent time on loan from [[Hull KR|Craven Park]] at the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>[https://hullkr.co.uk/news/hull-kr-duo-louix-gorman-and-leon-ruan-join-salford-red-devils-on-short-term-loan Hull KR duo Louix Gorman and Leon Ruan join Salford Red Devils on short-term loan]</ref>

Ruan joined the [[Bradford Bulls]] on a season-long loan from Hull Kingston Rovers for the [[2026 Super League season]].<ref>[https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/hull-kr-forward-leon-ruan-10713538 Hull KR forward Leon Ruan completes Super League move]</ref>
